# Implementation Completeness Checklist: Versioned Serialization + Checkpoint/Resume + Artifact Export

**Purpose**: Validate that all spec requirements have been correctly and completely implemented
**Created**: 2026-02-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md) | [data-model.md](../data-model.md) | [contracts/](../contracts/)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist.comp` command based on feature spec, contracts, data model, and implementation source code.

## Save and Load a Checkpoint (US1)

- [ ] CHK001 Is FR-001 fully implemented: `TrainingCheckpoint` contains all required fields — population (`IReadOnlyList<Genome>`), species assignments and metadata (Id, RepresentativeIndex, BestFitnessEver, GenerationsSinceImprovement, MemberIndices, MemberFitnesses), innovation counter state (NextInnovationNumber, NextNodeId), NextSpeciesId, champion (genome, fitness, generation), configuration snapshot, seed, RNG state (SeedArray[56], Inext, Inextp), generation number, run history, and artifact metadata? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK002 Is FR-002 fully implemented: `CheckpointSerializer.LoadAsync` deserializes and restores the complete training state such that all fields match the state at time of saving — `CheckpointDto.ToDomain()` maps population genomes, species metadata, innovation counters, champion, configuration, seed, RNG state, generation number, and run history back to a `TrainingCheckpoint`? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK003 Is FR-003 fully implemented: `ICheckpointSerializer.SaveAsync` and `LoadAsync` both accept `Stream` parameters, with no filesystem path or `FileStream` hard-coded anywhere in the serialization layer? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK004 Does `CheckpointDto.ToDto(TrainingCheckpoint)` correctly map the champion genome by index into the population (with structural equality fallback), and handle the edge case where champion is not in the population (extinct scenario) by serializing the champion genome standalone? [Correctness, Contract §ICheckpointSerializer, Data Model §CheckpointDto]
- [ ] CHK005 Does `CheckpointDto.ToDomain()` correctly reconstruct the champion genome — using the population index when available, falling back to the standalone genome DTO, and falling back to population[0] as a last resort — with an exception for empty population with no standalone champion? [Correctness, Data Model §CheckpointDto]
- [ ] CHK006 Does `SpeciesCheckpoint` correctly reference genomes by index into the population list (not by embedded genome copies) as specified in the data model? [Correctness, Data Model §SpeciesCheckpoint]

## Resume Training from Checkpoint (US2)

- [ ] CHK007 Is FR-004 fully implemented: `NeatEvolver.RunAsync` with `EvolutionRunOptions.ResumeFrom` restores population, species (reconstructing `Species` with Id, representative, BestFitnessEver, GenerationsSinceImprovement, and members with fitnesses), innovation counters via `IInnovationTracker.RestoreState`, species counter via `ISpeciationStrategy.RestoreState`, RNG state via `RngStateHelper.Restore`, generation counter, and champion — then resumes with deterministic continuation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK008 Is FR-005 fully implemented: the `OnCheckpoint` callback is only invoked at generation boundaries (after speciation, before reproduction/stopping check) in `NeatEvolver.RunAsync`, and no API exposes partial evaluation state? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK009 Is FR-006 fully implemented: cancellation via `CancellationToken` during a resumed run is checked at generation boundaries (at the top of the `while(true)` loop) with the same semantics as a fresh run, returning best result so far with `WasCancelled = true`? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK010 Is FR-006a fully implemented: `NeatEvolver.RunAsync` computes the current config hash via `ConfigurationHasher.ComputeHash` and compares it to `checkpoint.ConfigurationHash`, throwing `CheckpointException` on any mismatch before training resumes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006a]
- [ ] CHK011 Does the resume path in `NeatEvolver` correctly handle the edge case where the checkpoint was saved at the final generation (stopping criteria already met) — returning a result immediately without performing additional reproduction? [Correctness, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK012 Does the resume path correctly replay the reproduction step from the checkpointed generation (checkpoint saves after speciation, so resume must replay reproduction, complexity limits, and `NextGeneration()` before entering the main loop)? [Correctness, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Key Design Decision 5]

## Export Champion for Interoperability (US3)

- [ ] CHK013 Is FR-010 fully implemented: `ChampionExporter` produces a JSON document with a `nodes` array (each with `id`, `type` as lowercase string, `activationFunction`) and an `edges` array (each with `source`, `target`, `weight`, `enabled`)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK014 Is FR-011 fully implemented: the champion export JSON uses plain property names (`id`, `type`, `source`, `target`, `weight`, `enabled`) without NEATSharp-specific type information, making it parseable by any standard JSON reader? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011]
- [ ] CHK015 Is FR-012 fully implemented: the champion export includes full artifact metadata (library version, schema version, seed, configuration hash, creation timestamp UTC, environment) plus champion-specific fields (`fitness`, `generationFound`)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK016 Does `IChampionExporter` expose both overloads: `ExportAsync(Stream, EvolutionResult, CancellationToken)` and `ExportAsync(Stream, TrainingCheckpoint, CancellationToken)` as defined in the contract? [Correctness, Contract §IChampionExporter]
- [ ] CHK017 Does the `EvolutionResult` overload correctly access the champion genotype (throwing `InvalidOperationException` if null) and pass an empty string for `configurationHash` since `EvolutionResult` does not carry config hash? [Correctness, Contract §IChampionExporter]

## Version Artifacts and Detect Incompatibilities (US4)

- [ ] CHK018 Is FR-007 fully implemented: every serialized artifact (checkpoint via `CheckpointDto`, champion export via `ChampionExportDto`, diagnostics bundle via `DiagnosticsBundleDto`) includes a `schemaVersion` field set to `SchemaVersion.Current` ("1.0.0") following Semantic Versioning? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK019 Is FR-008 fully implemented: `CheckpointSerializer.LoadAsync` detects schema version mismatches and throws `SchemaVersionException` with `ArtifactVersion`, `ExpectedVersion`, and `IsMigrationAvailable` properties, producing an actionable human-readable error message that distinguishes newer-than-current from older-than-supported artifacts? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK020 Does `SchemaVersionException` correctly determine version directionality in its error message — identifying artifacts from newer library versions ("Update the library to load this artifact") vs. unsupported older versions ("Re-export the artifact with the current library version")? [Correctness, Spec §FR-008]

## Backward Compatibility with Prior Schema Versions (US5/US6)

- [ ] CHK021 Is FR-009 fully implemented: `ISchemaVersionMigrator` interface with `CanMigrate(string)` and `Migrate(JsonDocument, string)` methods is defined, and `SchemaVersionMigrator` composite class is implemented with an empty registry for v1.0.0? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK022 Is FR-009a infrastructure present: the migration code path in `CheckpointSerializer.LoadAsync` routes through `SchemaVersionMigrator` when `SchemaVersion.NeedsMigration` returns true and `_migrator.CanMigrate` returns true, with the explicit per-field default mapping expected to be implemented in version-specific migrators? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009a]
- [ ] CHK023 Does `SchemaVersion.IsCompatible` correctly determine version compatibility — returning true for versions between `MinimumSupported` and `Current` inclusive, and false for versions outside that range? [Correctness, Data Model §SchemaVersion]
- [ ] CHK024 Does `SchemaVersion.NeedsMigration` correctly identify versions that are older than `Current` but still within the supported range, returning false for the current version and for versions outside the supported range? [Correctness, Data Model §SchemaVersion]

## Create Diagnostics Bundle for Bug Reports (US5)

- [ ] CHK025 Is FR-016 fully implemented: `DiagnosticsBundleDto.ToDto(TrainingCheckpoint)` assembles a bundle containing a full checkpoint DTO, configuration (`NeatSharpOptions`), environment metadata (`EnvironmentInfo` with OsDescription, RuntimeVersion, Architecture via `CreateCurrent()`), and run history? [Completeness, Spec §FR-016]
- [ ] CHK026 Is FR-017 fully implemented: `IDiagnosticsBundleCreator.CreateAsync(Stream, TrainingCheckpoint, CancellationToken)` is a single method call that produces the complete diagnostics bundle? [Completeness, Spec §FR-017]

## Artifact Metadata

- [ ] CHK027 Is FR-013 fully implemented: `ArtifactMetadata` record contains all required fields — `SchemaVersion`, `LibraryVersion`, `Seed`, `ConfigurationHash`, `CreatedAtUtc` (ISO 8601 UTC string), and `Environment` (`EnvironmentInfo`) — and is included in every artifact type (checkpoint, champion export, diagnostics bundle)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK028 Is FR-014 fully implemented: `ConfigurationHasher.ComputeHash(NeatSharpOptions)` serializes options to JSON with camelCase naming and enum-as-string, then computes SHA-256, returning a 64-character lowercase hexadecimal string? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK029 Is FR-015 fully implemented: `CreatedAtUtc` is generated via `DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("O")` producing an ISO 8601 UTC string? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015]

## Storage Abstraction

- [ ] CHK030 Is FR-018 fully implemented: all serialization interfaces (`ICheckpointSerializer`, `IChampionExporter`, `IDiagnosticsBundleCreator`) use `Stream` parameters and none reference filesystem paths, `FileStream`, or `File` classes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018]
- [ ] CHK031 Is FR-019 fully implemented: all JSON serialization uses `System.Text.Json` (`JsonSerializer.SerializeAsync`/`DeserializeAsync`) with consistent options (camelCase, indented, enum-as-string via `JsonStringEnumConverter`) and no other serialization library is used? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019]
- [ ] CHK032 Is FR-020 fully implemented: DTOs exist for all required domain types — `GenomeDto` (with `NodeGeneDto`, `ConnectionGeneDto`), `SpeciesCheckpointDto`, `CheckpointDto` (with `CountersDto`, `ChampionDto`), `ArtifactMetadataDto`, `RngStateDto`, and `NeatSharpOptions`/`RunHistory` serialized directly — with bidirectional mapping (`ToDto`/`ToDomain`) methods? [Completeness, Spec §FR-020]

## Observability

- [ ] CHK033 Is FR-021 fully implemented: `SerializationLog.ConfigHashMismatch` emits a Warning-level log message when a configuration hash mismatch is detected during checkpoint load, and the migration-defaults warning path is present in the migration infrastructure (to be exercised when actual migrations exist)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021]
- [ ] CHK034 Is FR-022 fully implemented: `SerializationLog.IncompatibleSchemaVersion` emits an Error-level log message on incompatible schema version, `SerializationLog.CheckpointValidationFailed` emits Error-level on corruption, and no Info-level log messages are emitted during normal save/load operations? [Completeness, Spec §FR-022]

## Load Validation (FR-023)

- [ ] CHK035 Is FR-023(a) fully implemented: `CheckpointValidator.Validate` verifies that all connection genes in each genome reference nodes that exist in that genome's node list? **NOTE: Current implementation does NOT check per-genome node-connection reference integrity — only species indices, counter consistency, and champion presence are validated.** [Completeness, Spec §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK036 Is FR-023(b) fully implemented: `CheckpointValidator.ValidateSpeciesIndices` verifies that species representative and member indices are valid population indices (not negative and not >= population size)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK037 Is FR-023(c) fully implemented: `CheckpointValidator.ValidateCounterConsistency` verifies that `NextInnovationNumber` is strictly greater than the maximum innovation number across all genomes, and `NextNodeId` is strictly greater than the maximum node ID? [Completeness, Spec §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK038 Is FR-023(d) fully implemented: `CheckpointValidator.ValidateChampionInPopulation` verifies the champion genome exists in the population (by reference equality, then structural equality fallback), skipping the check for empty populations? [Completeness, Spec §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK039 Is FR-023(e) fully implemented: `CheckpointValidator.ValidateCounterConsistency` verifies that `NextSpeciesId` is strictly greater than the maximum species ID in the species list? [Completeness, Spec §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK040 Does `CheckpointValidator.Validate` collect all errors into a single `CheckpointValidationResult` rather than stopping at the first validation failure? [Correctness, Contract §ICheckpointValidator]

## Success Criteria Verification

- [ ] CHK041 Is SC-001 testable: save a checkpoint at generation N, load it, resume for M more generations, and the result (champion, fitness history, final population) is identical to an uninterrupted run of N+M generations with the same seed and configuration on the same CPU? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK042 Is SC-002 testable: checkpoint round-trip preserves all training state — population genomes (every node and connection), species metadata (Id, representative, BestFitnessEver, GenerationsSinceImprovement), innovation counters (next innovation number, next node ID), champion, seed, RNG state, generation number, and run history? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK043 Is SC-003 testable: champion export is a valid JSON document parseable by any standard JSON reader (e.g., `JsonDocument` without NEATSharp DTO types), and the parsed graph structure (nodes with ids/types/activation functions, edges with source/target/weight/enabled) matches the original genome exactly? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK044 Is SC-004 testable: loading an artifact with a mismatched schema version produces a `SchemaVersionException` (not a generic `JsonException`) that identifies the artifact's version, the expected version, and whether migration is available? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK045 Is SC-005 testable: a diagnostics bundle contains a checkpoint section, configuration, environment metadata (OsDescription, RuntimeVersion, Architecture), and run history — all extractable and parseable? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK046 Is SC-006 testable: every serialized artifact (checkpoint, champion export, diagnostics bundle) includes complete metadata — schema version, library version, seed, configuration hash, creation timestamp (UTC ISO 8601), and environment info? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK047 Is SC-007 testable: all serialization operations work with `MemoryStream` (no filesystem dependency) — checkpoint save/load, champion export, and diagnostics bundle creation all succeed with in-memory streams? [Measurability, Spec §SC-007]
- [ ] CHK048 Is SC-008 testable: a backward-compatibility test validates that a v1.0.0 fixture file loads correctly via `CheckpointSerializer.LoadAsync`, and a placeholder test structure exists for future v1→v2 migration? [Measurability, Spec §SC-008]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK049 Is the corrupt checkpoint edge case handled: `CheckpointSerializer.LoadAsync` catches malformed JSON (via `JsonDocument.ParseAsync` throwing), missing required fields (via null DTO result), and structural validation failures (via `CheckpointValidator`) — all producing clear error messages via `CheckpointCorruptionException` or `CheckpointException` without crashing or returning partial state? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK050 Is the interrupted write edge case documented correctly: the library writes to the consumer-provided stream with no filesystem knowledge, and `quickstart.md` documents the recommended temporary-then-rename pattern for atomic file writes? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK051 Is the empty population edge case handled: `CheckpointSerializer` and `CheckpointDto` correctly serialize/deserialize an empty population with zero genomes and zero species, the champion is serialized standalone (via `ChampionDto.Genome`), and the validator skips the champion-in-population check? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK052 Is the save-mid-evaluation edge case handled: the `OnCheckpoint` callback is only invoked between generations (after speciation), not during evaluation, and no public API allows constructing a checkpoint during partial evaluation? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK053 Is the missing-fields-from-older-schema edge case handled: the migration infrastructure (`ISchemaVersionMigrator`) supports defining per-field defaults in version-specific migrators, with the expectation of Warning-level logging when defaults are applied (infrastructure ready, not yet exercised for v1.0.0)? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK054 Is the different-configuration-on-resume edge case handled: `NeatEvolver.RunAsync` compares config hashes and throws `CheckpointException` with an actionable message identifying both hashes, before any training state is modified? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Configuration & Validation

- [ ] CHK055 Is `SchemaVersion.Current` correctly set to `"1.0.0"` and `SchemaVersion.MinimumSupported` to `"1.0.0"` as specified? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions, Data Model §SchemaVersion]
- [ ] CHK056 Does `RngState` enforce validation rules: `SeedArray` must have exactly 56 elements, `Inext` in [0, 55], `Inextp` in [0, 55] — throwing `ArgumentException` on violation? [Completeness, Data Model §RngState]
- [ ] CHK057 Does `EnvironmentInfo.CreateCurrent()` correctly capture `RuntimeInformation.OSDescription`, `RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription`, and `RuntimeInformation.ProcessArchitecture.ToString()`? [Completeness, Data Model §EnvironmentInfo]
- [ ] CHK058 Does `ArtifactMetadata` correctly store all six required fields: SchemaVersion, LibraryVersion, Seed, ConfigurationHash, CreatedAtUtc (ISO 8601 UTC), and Environment (EnvironmentInfo)? [Completeness, Data Model §ArtifactMetadata]

## Cross-Spec Integration

- [ ] CHK059 Is `IInnovationTracker` from prior specs correctly extended: `NextInnovationNumber` and `NextNodeId` read-only properties and `RestoreState(int, int)` method are added to the interface and implemented in `InnovationTracker`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Key Design Decision 7]
- [ ] CHK060 Is `ISpeciationStrategy` from prior specs correctly extended: `NextSpeciesId` read-only property and `RestoreState(int)` method are added to the interface and implemented in `CompatibilitySpeciation`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Key Design Decision 7]
- [ ] CHK061 Does `NeatEvolver` call `IInnovationTracker.RestoreState` and `ISpeciationStrategy.RestoreState` through the interface abstractions (not downcasting to concrete types) during the resume path? [Consistency, Plan §Key Design Decision 7]
- [ ] CHK062 Is `INeatEvolver` correctly extended with the `RunAsync(IEvaluationStrategy, EvolutionRunOptions, CancellationToken)` overload while keeping the existing simple `RunAsync(IEvaluationStrategy, CancellationToken)` overload backward-compatible (delegating to the new overload with default `EvolutionRunOptions`)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Key Design Decision 5]
- [ ] CHK063 Does the `EvolutionResult` type expose the champion's `Genotype` (nullable `Genome`) so that `ChampionExporter` can access the raw genome for export from an `EvolutionResult`? [Consistency, Contract §IChampionExporter]

## DI Wiring & Service Registration

- [ ] CHK064 Are all serialization services registered in `ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddNeatSharp()`: `ICheckpointSerializer` → `CheckpointSerializer` (Singleton), `ICheckpointValidator` → `CheckpointValidator` (Singleton), `ISchemaVersionMigrator` → `SchemaVersionMigrator` (Singleton), `IChampionExporter` → `ChampionExporter` (Singleton), `IDiagnosticsBundleCreator` → `DiagnosticsBundleCreator` (Singleton)? [Completeness, Tasks §T037, §T045, §T052]
- [ ] CHK065 Does `CheckpointSerializer` accept `ICheckpointValidator`, `ISchemaVersionMigrator`, and `ILogger<CheckpointSerializer>` via constructor injection (with a convenience overload using `NullLogger` when no logger is configured)? [Completeness, Plan §DI]

## Data Model Completeness

- [ ] CHK066 Does the `EvolutionRunOptions` class expose exactly the fields specified: `ResumeFrom` (`TrainingCheckpoint?`) and `OnCheckpoint` (`Func<TrainingCheckpoint, CancellationToken, Task>?`)? [Completeness, Data Model §EvolutionRunOptions]
- [ ] CHK067 Does `CheckpointException` correctly inherit from `NeatSharpException` and provide both `(string message)` and `(string message, Exception innerException)` constructors? [Completeness, Data Model §Exception Types]
- [ ] CHK068 Does `CheckpointCorruptionException` correctly inherit from `CheckpointException`, expose `IReadOnlyList<string> ValidationErrors`, and format a human-readable message listing all validation errors? [Completeness, Data Model §Exception Types]
- [ ] CHK069 Does `SchemaVersionException` correctly inherit from `CheckpointException` and expose `ArtifactVersion`, `ExpectedVersion`, and `IsMigrationAvailable` properties? [Completeness, Data Model §Exception Types]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline when an item reveals a gap
- Items reference spec sections (`Spec §FR-XXX`), success criteria (`Spec §SC-XXX`), contracts (`Contract §TypeName`), data model (`Data Model §Section`), or tasks (`Tasks §TXXX`)
- **CHK035 flags a potential implementation gap**: FR-023(a) requires validating that all connection genes reference existing nodes in each genome, but `CheckpointValidator` does not currently perform this check
- 69 total items covering 23 functional requirements, 8 success criteria, 6 edge cases
- Traceability: 100% of items include at least one source reference
